title: Lost Package
steps: Contact your proxy service first — they can trace the package on their end.
Check tracking number on the carrier website (DHL, FedEx, EMS, etc.).
If no update for 7+ days, ask your proxy to file a trace request with the carrier.
File a claim with the shipping carrier if the package is confirmed lost.
If you purchased proxy insurance, submit your claim through the proxy's insurance process.

title: Customs Hold
steps: Check your tracking — customs holds usually show as 'Held at customs' or 'Awaiting clearance'.
Provide the commercial invoice or purchase receipt to customs if requested.
Contact the shipping carrier for customs clearance assistance.
Pay any assessed duties or taxes — DHL/FedEx typically send an invoice.
For future shipments, ensure accurate value declarations to avoid delays.

title: Wrong Item Received
steps: Take clear photos of what you received vs. what you ordered.
Contact your proxy within 3 days of delivery — most have strict claim windows.
Request a return or refund through the proxy's dispute process.
The proxy will contact the original seller on your behalf.
Keep the item and all packaging until the dispute is resolved.

title: Damaged Item
steps: Photograph the damage immediately during unboxing (video is even better).
File a damage claim with your proxy service.
If you purchased shipping insurance, submit photos as evidence for the claim.
Contact the carrier directly if the proxy does not resolve it.
Keep all original packaging — carriers may require inspection.

title: Shipping Delays
steps: Check carrier tracking for the latest status update.
Contact your proxy for an update if tracking hasn't moved in 5+ days.
Be aware of seasonal delays: Dec-Jan (holidays) and late Apr-May (Golden Week).
EMS and SAL are slower than DHL/FedEx — expect 1-4 weeks depending on method.
Consider choosing DHL or FedEx next time for faster, more reliable delivery.

title: Fee Disputes
steps: Screenshot the original listing price and any fee estimates shown before purchase.
Compare with your final invoice from the proxy.
Contact support with your evidence — most proxies have live chat.
If chat support doesn't resolve it, escalate via email with screenshots attached.
Check the proxy's terms of service for their dispute resolution policy.
